frame_analyses.description: Instructor narration: 'Pull back the left instrument (camera) to start the exercise.' Yellow circle pattern overlay visible on liver model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This is an instructional/tutorial video with explicit verbal cues and overlays

frame_analyses.description: Yellow circle pattern shown with cardinal mark indicators (top, left, right, bottom). Green circle overlay indicates target area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates the 4cm diameter circular cutting target with 2mm accuracy requirement

frame_analyses.description: Yellow dashed circle pattern shown in progress. Instructor narration: 'Find the left side of the liver.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper target identification on anatomy model

frame_analyses.description: Green circle overlay on gauze suspended between clamps. Instructor identifies: 'Find the gallbladder' and 'Find the pyloric sphincter.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates identification of anatomical landmarks on the gauze cutting target

frame_analyses.description: Green circle overlay on liver model showing 'Find the pyloric sphincter' with anatomical context.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Comprehensive instructional content identifying key anatomical structures

frame_analyses.description: Yellow circle pattern overlay on gauze with instruction 'Find the colon's hepatic (or right) flexure.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates target structure orientation and naming

frame_analyses.description: Instruction overlay: 'Press pedal to exit.' Yellow circle target on gauze. Shows cutting preparation phase.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional cue for transitioning out of setup phase

frame_analyses.description: Multiple yellow circle patterns shown on gauze. Instruction: 'Find the right side of the falciform ligament.' Shows ascending colon with yellow circle overlay.
Yellow circle overlay with instruction: 'Press pedal to exit.' Yellow pattern shown around kidney anatomy.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final anatomical landmark identification before task execution

frame_analyses.description: Green circle overlay on complete view. Camera instruction: 'Hold the camera steady until the next target is selected.' Yellow circle shown on gauze model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Camera positioning and task readiness instructions

frame_analyses.description: Yellow circle pattern shown on liver model with full anatomical context visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Summary view showing complete task setup and target area

frame_analyses.description: Final view of yellow circle target on gauze with all anatomical landmarks labeled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional video demonstrates proper task setup and anatomical identification without performing the cutting task

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/tutorial video with explicit narration, overlay graphics (yellow and green circles), and step-by-step anatomical identification cues. No actual task performance is demonstrated. Per scoring rubric, instructional content should NOT be scored as a performance — only teaching content should be extracted.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no actual cutting task performance shown
Penalties — no cutting attempted, no deviation or errors to measure
technique_summary: This is an expert-level instructional demonstration explaining Task 2 (Pattern Cut) setup, target identification, anatomical landmarks, and camera positioning. No cutting performance is shown.
strengths: Clear verbal cues and anatomical landmark identification
Effective use of overlay graphics (yellow and green circles) to highlight target areas
Comprehensive coverage of proper task setup and camera positioning

demonstrated_techniques: Proper identification of circular cutting target (4cm diameter)
Anatomical landmark recognition: gallbladder, pyloric sphincter, colon flexure, falciform ligament, kidney
Camera positioning and steadiness during task setup
Use of pedalto exit and enter task mode
verbal_cues: Pull back the left instrument (camera) to start the exercise
Find the left side of the liver
Find the colon's hepatic (or right) flexure
Find the right side of the falciform ligament
Hold the camera steady until the next target is selected
common_errors_addressed: Improper anatomical orientation (instructional cues guide correct identification)
Poor camera positioning (explicitly instructs to keep camera steady)
Missing target identification steps (comprehensive walkthrough of all landmarks)

improvement_suggestions: No performance scored — this is an instructional video only
